description: Canonical shared vocabulary for the Colaberry Trust Before Intelligence program. Enforces GOALS-Lexicon: terminology must not drift across AI artifacts. Checked deterministically by execution/ops_platform/lexicon.py and gated in CI by scripts/lexicon_check.py. 'terms' are the preferred vocabulary (aliases flag non-canonical drift = warning); 'forbidden' are banned/deprecated terms (= blocking). 'allow_in' lists repo-relative paths where a literal mention is legitimate.

terms.definition: The mandatory compliance framework: stand up the trust foundation BEFORE deploying intelligence. No AI artifact ships without a passing TBI attestation.
The per-artifact sidecar (<artifact>.tbi.json) proving an AI artifact satisfies INPACT + GOALS + the 7-layer mapping; scored by the deterministic scorer and enforced by the CI gate.
The six interaction dimensions every AI artifact must satisfy or justify n_a: Instant, Natural, Permitted, Adaptive, Contextual, Transparent.
The five trust properties (v1.1 canonical wording): Governance, Observability, Availability, Lexicon, Solid.
The trust stack from L1 Multi-Modal Storage through L7; each artifact maps which layers it relies on.
A human-readable SOP/runbook under /directives describing goals, inputs, outputs, edge cases, and how success is verified.
An autonomous code entrypoint that takes action on live data (Tier 0), declared in config/tbi_runtime_agents.json and gated by its own attestation.
An operator control (global or per-agent) that the live workers obey at call-time via runtime_controls to halt autonomy.
TBI verdict: every INPACT/GOALS dimension satisfied (no blocking issues, no caveats).
TBI verdict: passes the gate but carries justified n_a dimensions or a trust caution.
TBI verdict: a blocking issue is present; the CI gate fails the PR.
An INPACT/GOALS dimension marked not-applicable; allowed ONLY with written evidence justifying it, or the gate fails.
The super-admin dashboard at /admin/trust: live 7-layer diagram, autonomy controls (kill-switches), and the cost / availability / runtime-trust / lexicon signals.
The mandatory failure response: find root cause, fix logic, add/extend tests, update the directive, confirm the system is stronger.

forbidden.reason: This project does not use Moltbot; Claude Code is the build/validation agent, not the runtime executor (CLAUDE.md). Naming it in an AI artifact signals a stale mental model.
The deprecated Basecamp '+ai' persona connect step (the Obi/Ram trap). Operators connect their HUMAN Basecamp account; AI personas follow the {name}-ai convention, not a '+ai' account.
